spec_match.rationale: The paired iframes still differ only by `allow="focus-without-user-activation 'none'"`, and each frame now reports its own `permissionsPolicy` or `featurePolicy` state for the focus policy.

interactive.rationale: Parent controls and iframe controls exercise multiple focus paths, frame results are posted back live, and a fixed parent `document.activeElement` readout updates continuously.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature-index promise to watch a focus indicator update is now fulfilled by the parent focus readout and per-frame event log.

edge_cases.rationale: The page covers `element.focus()`, `window.focus()`, generated `autofocus`, `dialog.showModal()`, `showPopover()`, and direct user click focus inside both frames.

references.rationale: The see-also section keeps the ChromeStatus link and now also cites the HTML focus policy anchor and W3C policy explainer.

design_system.rationale: The unrelated generic live-probe panel was removed and the concept-specific frame styles use the same design-system token values instead of arbitrary probe boilerplate.

summary: The focus hijack guard now has a real parent active-element indicator, policy-specific iframe reports, controls for every focus path called out by the feature, user-initiated focus proof, and no generic capability-probe panel.
